title: Will President Trump send ICE agents to Boston’s Logan Airport?
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: President Trump plans to order ICE agents to airports to address staffing shortages due to a government shutdown.
Logan Airport has not experienced delays seen elsewhere, and officials have not confirmed ICE deployment there.
TSA union leaders in New England are making inquiries but have heard no official plans involving ICE at Logan Airport.

title: Is Bruins goalie Jeremy Swayman a legitimate Vezina candidate? The numbers speak for themselves.
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jeremy Swayman helped the Boston Bruins secure a crucial 4-2 win against the Detroit Red Wings with 42 saves.
Boston Bruins' playoff chances improved after the victory, raising odds to 78.8% according to MoneyPuck.
Swayman ranks second in NHL with 27.1 goals saved above expected, bolstering his Vezina Trophy candidacy.

title: Celtics legend Paul Pierce continues to make a case for Jaylen Brown as NBA MVP
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Paul Pierce advocates for Jaylen Brown as a deserving NBA MVP candidate due to his standout performance this season.
Jaylen Brown averages career-highs with 28.5 points, 7.0 rebounds, and 5.2 assists per game during Jayson Tatum's injury absence.
Despite team success, Paul Pierce believes Celtics players like Brown and Tatum often lack individual recognition in the NBA.

title: Plymouth police officer on paid leave amid criminal investigation
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Plymouth police officer Samantha Pelrine placed on paid leave amid an ongoing criminal investigation by State Police.
The investigation concerns Pelrine's off-duty conduct, prompting an internal review by Plymouth Police Department.
Plymouth County District Attorney's office and State Police declined to provide further details on the investigation.

title: MLB writer predicts Roman Anthony will win AL MVP this season
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: MLB writer Eno Sarris predicts Roman Anthony will win the AL MVP in the 2026 season.
Anthony impressed in the 2026 World Baseball Classic with Team USA, batting .280 with two home runs.
Anthony ended the 2025 MLB season with a .292 average, eight home runs, and 32 RBI over 71 games.

title: Pedestrian killed in Roxbury hit-and-run
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: A pedestrian was killed in a hit-and-run on Friday morning on Southampton and Bradston Streets in Roxbury.
The victim was pronounced dead at a local hospital after the incident, according to Boston police.
The driver fled the scene, and although the vehicle was found, no arrests have been made as the investigation continues.

title: Here’s what meteorologists are saying about chances for snow through Monday
author: Jack Pickell
bullets: National Weather Service predicts up to 2 inches of wet snow near I-90 by Monday night.
Rain expected to switch to wet snow and sleet from northern MA to CT, RI, and SE MA early Monday.
Meteorologists anticipate a coating to 2 inches of snow, with grassy areas seeing minor accumulation.

title: Mass. police officer arrested for OUI, department says
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Norwood Police Officer Shawn Willman was arrested on an OUI charge while off duty, department announced Sunday.
Willman is on administrative leave and his employment depends on the outcome of the criminal case.
Norwood Police Department is conducting an internal affairs investigation following the arrest of Officer Willman.

title: Police: Mass. man allegedly stabbed friend during fight over woman at Miami Beach bachelor party
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Olajuwon Shawn Dickerson, 32, of Marston Mills, is charged with aggravated battery for allegedly stabbing a friend in Miami Beach.
The incident occurred during a fight over attention from women at a bachelor party on Ocean Drive, Miami Beach.
Both men were hospitalized for their injuries and police recovered a blood-covered folding knife at the scene.

title: 4 takeaways from the Revolution’s 3-1 loss to St. Louis City SC
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: The New England Revolution lost 3-1 to St. Louis City SC despite leading early with a goal from Ilay Feingold.
St. Louis City SC capitalized on a set piece, scoring the go-ahead goal right before halftime, exposing New England's defensive gaps.
Forward Leo Campana suffered a new injury during the match, requiring further evaluation after leaving the game in the 80th minute.
